verdict_reasoning: SRC is a canonical cytoplasmic non-receptor tyrosine kinase with no inherent membrane anchor. However, it has two well-documented surface contexts: (1) myristoylation-driven palmitoylation anchors it to the inner leaflet of the PM, where it engages with receptor tyrosine kinases and integrins in signaling complexes; (2) oncogenic transformation drives relocalization of SRC to the outer leaflet in tumor cells (cancer ecto-kinase), a phenomenon extensively documented in the literature. The baseline compartment is cytoplasmic, but the PM-associated pools — both inner-leaflet signaling and cancer-cell ecto-display — are documented and functionally relevant. This qualifies as `contextual` via dual localization (PM signaling minority pool with its own lipid anchor) and cell-state induction (cancer ecto-kinase display).
predicted_key_uncertainty: Whether the inner-leaflet myristoylated PM pool is large/stable enough to count independently of cancer-driven ecto-display. SRC is canonically cytoplasmic but engages PM signaling complexes; the cancer ecto-pool is well-documented but context-restricted. Both qualify for `contextual`, but the degree to which baseline signaling involves a stable/substantial PM pool vs. transient recruitment is unresolved.
